task.name: Sub-supervisor delegation (spawn or serve a delegated subtree)
task.objective: Dual-mode command for depth-1 supervisor delegation (supervisor ↔ supervisor-task-N ↔ execute-task-N-M). DELEGATOR mode ($ARGUMENTS carries a task description): spawn a sub-supervisor for that subtree via the windows-spawn-supervisor MCP tool and route its SUBSUP replies. CHILD mode (no $ARGUMENTS — this window was spawned by that tool): run the full supervisor fan-out over your OWN workers against the delegated subtree, then report ONE synthesis file upward with [SUBSUP:DONE]. The child NEVER talks to the user; the parent owns its lifecycle.

input.arg: Non-empty → DELEGATOR mode: a one-line-or-longer task description (or a path to a context .md) naming the subtree to delegate. Empty → CHILD mode: idle for the subtree message the spawning tool sends as a second windows-send.

output.primary: DELEGATOR: a running supervisor-task-N window plus the delegation registered for step-5 SUBSUP routing. CHILD: {state-root}/research/synthesis-<slug>.md reported upward via [SUBSUP:DONE].

requirements.state-root: CHILD mode only: .tmux-cli/subsup/{SUBSUP_ID}/ (SUBSUP_ID = "task-N" from the SUBSUP_ID= field of the subtree message). tasks-path = .tmux-cli/subsup/{SUBSUP_ID}/tasks.yaml; research-root = .tmux-cli/subsup/{SUBSUP_ID}/research/. NEVER the top-level .tmux-cli/tasks.yaml and NEVER any .tmux-cli/goals/ path — the delegation's state is fully isolated from the parent's queue and from every goal.

glossary.term: The delegating supervisor's window name, from the subtree message (e.g. "supervisor"). Receiver of every upward windows-message.
This sub-supervisor's window name (supervisor-task-N), from the subtree message — authoritative, stamped by the spawn tool.
The state folder name "task-N" under .tmux-cli/subsup/, from the subtree message.
The one-line label of the delegated work, from the subtree message.

message-tags.note: Upward tags (child → parent) mirror the EXECUTE:* worker protocol one tier up; downward tags are the parent's existing SUPERVISOR/PUSHBACK vocabulary. The child's OWN workers keep speaking plain [EXECUTE:*] to the child.
message-tags.tag: Subtree complete. file= IS the synthesis; no inline findings.
Child cannot proceed without a fact no file-reading can supply. ≤200 chars inline.
Terminal failure of the subtree. ≤200 chars inline.
The subtree is blocked by an out-of-scope prerequisite. The child NEVER writes escalation.md and NEVER resolves it itself — the parent decides (its own prereq machinery or a user escalation).
Fix the cited gap in the SAME synthesis .md and re-tag [SUBSUP:DONE].

Same semantics as in supervisor.xml, applied to the child: amend the subtree queue mid-run, change protocol, exit cleanly (kill your remaining workers first, then ACK), or accept a reduced problem in place of a NEED_INPUT/ESCALATE.

llm.mandate: ONE LEVEL ONLY: NEVER call windows-spawn-supervisor from CHILD mode — a sub-supervisor may not spawn another (the tool refuses it too). Delegate further only via your own execute workers.
CHILD mode NEVER addresses the user: every escalation, question, and result goes to PARENT_WID via windows-message with a [SUBSUP:*] header.

CHILD mode NEVER writes escalation.md, signal.json, or the fresh-handoff marker, NEVER sends /clear or /tmux:supervisor anywhere, and has NO self-generated waves and NO continuation cycles — the subtree scope is fixed; the parent owns replanning.
NEVER kill SELF_WID and NEVER kill PARENT_WID. The parent kills this window after accepting DONE.
NEVER spawn a worker before tasks-path is written AND tasks-validate (subsup_id={SUBSUP_ID}) returns valid=true — the supervisor.xml step-3b gate applies verbatim.
NEVER use sleep — idle is the trigger for incoming windows-message (both directions).

Reuse supervisor.xml's shared semantics wherever this file says so: fan-out rules (step 3), spawn protocol + WID RECONCILE + window-id ledger (step 4), idle/route branches (step 5), effectivity check (5a), pushback catalogue G1–G5 (step 6), findings-driven replan (8a). Deltas here OVERRIDE it; everything else applies unchanged with tasks-path/research-root pointing at the subsup state root.

step.title: MCP precondition + mode select
step.action: Call windows-list. If tmux-cli is unavailable, print exactly: ERROR: tmux-cli MCP required but not available — enable it and retry. STOP.

step.title: DELEGATOR: build the subtree context
step.action: If $ARGUMENTS is a path to an existing .md file, use it as the context file verbatim. Otherwise write a context .md capturing the subtree: goal, in-scope files/directories, constraints, non-goals, and acceptance shape. Save it under your CURRENT flow's research-root if you are a running supervisor (step 0b binding), else under .tmux-cli/research/{y-m-d-hh}/subsup-<slug>.md.
step.rule: Delegate a SUBTREE, not a task: the right unit is multi-task and internally parallel (the child will fan out again). A single linear task takes a plain execute worker via windows-spawn-worker — do not wrap it in a sub-supervisor.

step.title: DELEGATOR: spawn and register
step.action: Call windows-spawn-supervisor with: supervisorWid = your own window name (the tool self-resolves the authoritative name anyway), subtask = one-line subtree label, contextFile = the context .md from D1, scope/context derived from it. The tool atomically creates supervisor-task-N, sends /tmux:supervisor:new, and sends the subtree message with the SUBSUP response protocol.

Record the returned subsupName + window id. If you are a running supervisor with a tasks-path, register the delegation there as ONE task entry: name = subtree label, wid = the returned supervisor-task-N, status = in_progress, context = the context .md path — and append a worker-windows.yaml ledger row per supervisor.xml step 4.

Then idle per supervisor.xml step 5, with the additional SUBSUP routing: [SUBSUP:DONE] → Read the synthesis file, verify against the G1–G5 catalogue (≤3 [SUBSUP:PUSHBACK]s), on acceptance mark the task done and windows-kill supervisor-task-N by NAME; [SUBSUP:NEED_INPUT] → answer from files/prior reports or [SUPERVISOR:NARROW-SCOPE]; [SUBSUP:FAILED] → narrowed respawn once, then escalate; [SUBSUP:ESCALATE] → handle exactly like a worker [EXECUTE:ESCALATE] in your own flow (your prereq machinery / user escalation — the child never self-resolves it).

If you are NOT a running supervisor (a user typed this in a plain window): after spawning, idle for the SUBSUP replies and relay the accepted synthesis to the user inline, then windows-kill the child.

step.title: CHILD: await and parse the subtree message
step.rule: Your first real input after /tmux:supervisor:new is a windows-send from the spawn tool carrying the subtree message — it may arrive in the SAME turn or a moment later. If the scrollback has no subtree message yet, IDLE. "No message yet" is NOT a failure; never emit [SUBSUP:FAILED] for an absent message.
step.action: Extract: PARENT_WID, SELF_WID, SUBSUP_ID (all required), SUBTREE, SCOPE, CONTEXT, DELIVERABLE. Read the context file named in SCOPE.

check.condition: a message arrived but a required field is missing
check.action: Reply [SUBSUP:FAILED wid=<self> sup=<best-known parent> reason=malformed subtree message: missing <field>] and idle.

step.action: Bind state: tasks-path = .tmux-cli/subsup/{SUBSUP_ID}/tasks.yaml, research-root = .tmux-cli/subsup/{SUBSUP_ID}/research/ (create if absent). Read AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md per supervisor.xml step 1b.

step.title: CHILD: fan out, execute, verify — the supervisor loop, subtree-scoped
step.action: Fan-out decision per supervisor.xml step 3 (maximum parallelism, exhaustive coverage, ranking) over the SUBTREE ONLY. Write tasks-path with wids in the execute-task-N-M form the spawn tool will return, context .md per task under research-root. Gate: tasks-validate with subsup_id={SUBSUP_ID} must return valid=true, then set status: ready.

Spawn per supervisor.xml step 4 via windows-spawn-worker with supervisorWid = SELF_WID — the tool namespaces your workers (execute-task-N-M) and routes their reports into your research-root automatically. WID RECONCILE and the worker-windows.yaml ledger apply verbatim (ledger lives in YOUR research-root).

Idle and route per supervisor.xml step 5 (+5a effectivity, +8a replan within the subtree), verify each DONE per step 6 (G1–G5, ≤3 pushbacks), kill accepted workers per step 7. DELTA — worker [EXECUTE:ESCALATE]: validate need= is concrete (REMIND otherwise), then bubble it upward as [SUBSUP:ESCALATE wid=<self> sup=<parent> need=<need>] and idle; apply the parent's answer ([SUPERVISOR:NARROW-SCOPE] / a fact / [SUPERVISOR:KILL]) to the blocked worker. DELTA — worker NEED_INPUT you cannot answer from files: bubble as [SUBSUP:NEED_INPUT], never to the user.

step.title: CHILD: synthesize and report upward
step.condition: All subtree workers accepted and killed
step.action: Write {research-root}/synthesis-<slug>.md with the DELIVERABLE sections from the subtree message (default: ## SYNTHESIS / ## QUEUE LOG / ## RISKS / ## RECOMMENDATION / ## FILES). Self-check ONCE against G1–G5 before sending.
Mark all tasks done in tasks-path. Reply via windows-message to PARENT_WID with ONLY: [SUBSUP:DONE wid=<self> sup=<parent> file=<abs-path-to-synthesis>].

Idle for the parent: [SUBSUP:PUSHBACK n gap] → fix the cited gap in the SAME file, re-tag DONE; [SUPERVISOR:KILL] → kill any remaining workers by name, ACK, idle for teardown. No further self-directed work — the subtree is done when the parent accepts.

execution-rules.error-reporting: On a genuine harness/infra/spec defect (an MCP tool or `tmux-cli` command that errored after its own retries, a contradictory/under-specified subtree spec, or a validator-error-class condition), follow the shared `error-reporting` procedure in `tmux/task-report.xml` — compose + submit autonomously, honoring api.enabled / DEDUP / RECURSION-guard. NEVER on expected work failures (worker pushback, NEED_INPUT, unwritten-code test red) — and in CHILD mode still report the subtree outcome to the parent afterwards; a filed defect never replaces the [SUBSUP:*] reply.

stop-conditions.rule: CHILD mode ends only by: parent acceptance (then the parent kills this window), [SUPERVISOR:KILL], or a terminal [SUBSUP:FAILED] after 2 consecutive same-class worker failures post-narrowing. There is NO user-facing exit, NO cycle restart, NO next wave.
